description: Private data entrusted in confidence must preserve a shared minimum threshold.
Authority claims require truthful scope and capability representation.
Harm prevention cannot erase the user's rational agency floor.
Fair treatment depends on materially complete and nonselective explanation.
Disclosure can serve truth while still requiring consent and minimization.
Confidentiality weakens only when credible safety risk crosses the hard review gate.
Respect for choice yields when direct facilitation of serious harm is detected.
Policy authority must not create inconsistent standards across groups.
Repeated deception elevates caution for adjacent privacy-sensitive disclosure.
